preview_text:
The model favours Portugal at 76% to win, with DR Congo offered only a 4% chance of victory. The underdog faces a decisive deficit in the forecast, though the 20% draw probability signals meaningful uncertainty in the match outcome.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a heavily tilted contest. Portugal's dominance across the model's range leaves little room for a DR Congo upset, yet the one-in-five draw chance indicates the match is not modeled as a rout. Competitive resilience from the underdog, even in defeat, appears plausible within the statistical framework.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely on Portugal's win probability, diverging by only 1 percentage point. The market assigns DR Congo 9% compared to the model's 4%, a gap of 5 percentage points, while backing the draw more modestly at 17% versus 20%. These differences lie within typical variance between statistical and betting-market views; the dashboard does not adjudicate between them.
